behavior_summary: MAGARM::look (Event 0) makes the item produce a short descriptive bark 'magic armor ' when examined.

When an actor enters the item's fast-area trigger (enterFastArea), the script checks an item-local status bit (0x04). If that bit is clear it spawns a persistent behavior (U8P_MAGARM::00F4) to automate attacks/throws while the actor remains in range.
When the actor leaves the fast-area (leaveFastArea) a cleanup routine (FREE::2A56) is invoked to cancel or free resources/timers associated with the spawned behavior.

The spawned behavior U8P_MAGARM::00F4 clears the 'armed/active' status bit (0x04), repeatedly computes randomized target offsets within the item's range, validates candidate targets with Item::legal_move, and calls Item::hurl to launch the magic armor toward valid targets. It biases distances via multiple urandom calls, clamps offsets, and schedules a FREE::* timer (FREE::1C19) for delayed cleanup or re-arm before suspending/looping.
notable_control_flow: Event-driven: MAGARM::look responds to Event 0 by calling Item::bark and suspending.
Trigger-driven: U8P_MAGARM::enterFastArea tests Item::getStatus & 0x04; if clear, spawn U8P_MAGARM::00F4.
Cleanup-driven: U8P_MAGARM::leaveFastArea calls FREE::2A56(item, 0x00F0) to cancel/clean spawned state.

Behavior loop (U8P_MAGARM::00F4): clear status bit via Item::andStatus, read Item::getRange and coordinates, compute candidate dx/dy using urandom and helper FREE::1C52, clamp and sign-adjust offsets, call Item::legal_move until a legal target is found, then call Item::hurl; finally spawn FREE::1C19 (delay/cleanup) and suspend/loop.
open_questions: Exact semantic meanings of the item-local status bits (0x04, 0x08, 0x10) beyond their inferred roles (armed/active, alternate randomization branch) remain unconfirmed.

Precise timing and behavior of the spawned FREE::* helpers (FREE::1C19 and FREE::2A56) — whether they re-arm the item, free resources immediately, or schedule reactivation — is inferred but not certain.
Exact numeric bounds used for offset clamping (approx. ±64 inferred) and the interpretation of some temporary constants are approximate based on disassembly patterns.

Whether U8P_MAGARM::00F4 targets the avatar specifically or arbitrary nearby squares (using avatar-relative CX/CY vs. item X/Y) is inferred from calls to Item::getCX/getCY but not explicitly documented.

main_function.name: U8P_MAGARM::look
main_function.purpose: When the item is examined (U8P_MAGARM::look event) produce a short description/bark string identifying it as 'magic armor'.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_MAGARM::look) — triggered when a player or actor inspects the item.

function_map.name: U8P_MAGARM::look
function_map.role: Handle 'U8P_MAGARM::look' event: call Item::bark with the static string 'magic armor ' and suspend.

function_map.name: U8P_MAGARM::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area entry trigger: test item status bit 0x04 and spawn U8P_MAGARM::00F4 if the bit is clear.

function_map.name: U8P_MAGARM::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit trigger: invoke FREE::2A56 to cancel/cleanup timers or spawned behavior associated with the item.

function_map.name: U8P_MAGARM::00F4
function_map.role: Spawned persistent behavior: clear the 0x04 status bit, compute randomized target offsets within range, validate targets with Item::legal_move, call Item::hurl to throw the magic armor, and schedule FREE::1C19 before suspending/looping.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn the persistent attack/throw behavior when the item becomes active for a nearby actor.

external_calls.purpose: Display the item's short description 'magic armor ' to the inspecting process/player.
Read item status flags and test bit 0x04 to decide whether to spawn the behavior.
Engine spawn primitive used to start U8P_MAGARM::00F4 with the item as argument.
Cleanup/cancel resources or timers associated with the spawned behavior (called with item and a numeric argument).
Clear the item status bit 0x04 (bitwise-and with inverse mask) as the behavior begins.
Obtain the item's effective range value used as a maximum radius for computing target offsets.
Read a container/avatar-relative X coordinate used as the basis for some offset computations (inferred to bias toward the avatar).
Read a container/avatar-relative Y coordinate used as the basis for some offset computations (inferred to bias toward the avatar).
Read the item's world X coordinate to compute absolute target world X = item X + dx.
Read the item's world Y coordinate to compute absolute target world Y = item Y + dy.
Read the item's Z-plane/height for legal_move/hurl calls.
Generate random values used repeatedly to bias distance, sign, and perturbation of target offsets.
Small utility called to convert/compare random-derived values during offset selection and branching.
Check whether a computed WorldPoint is a legal target for throwing; reject and try alternate candidates if false.
Perform the throwing/hurling of the magic armor toward the validated target (passes dx, dy, dz and flags/speed).
Spawn FREE::1C19 (a timer/cleanup helper) after launching, likely to schedule re-arming or delayed cleanup.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: MAGARM::look (Event 0) makes the item produce a short descriptive bark 'magic armor ' when examined.
